Quote: Do you have any advice for your readers? Lead the life that's yours instead of faking someone else's.
Author: Sandy Nathan

Quote: Then I realize what it is. It's him. Something about him makes me feel like I am about to fall. Or turn to liquid. Or burst into flames.
Author: Veronica Roth

Quote: This is a time when all of God's people need to keep their eyes and their Bibles wide open. We must ask God for discernment as never before.
Author: David Jeremiah
Origin: Until Christ Returns: Living Faithfully Today While We Wait for Our Glorious Tomorrow

Quote: It's a lack of clarity that creates chaos and frustration. Those emotions are poison to any living goal.
Author: Steve Maraboli
Origin: Life, the Truth, and Being Free

Quote: My heart is warm with the friends I make, And better friends I'll not be knowing, Yet there isn't a train I wouldn't take, No matter where it's going.
Author: Edna St. Vincent Millay

Quote: People instantly assume you can't have a platonic friendship with someone of the opposite sex. I think this may be specific to L.A. - or America.
Author: Isabel Lucas

Quote: To see a World in a grain of sand, And a Heaven in a wild flower, Hold Infinity in the palm of your hand, And Eternity in an hour.
Author: William Blake

Quote: If there's a God out there, then i would hope he has more important things to attend to than my drinking scotch or eating pork.
Author: Khaled Hosseini
Origin: The Kite Runner: A Portrait of the Marc Forster Film

Quote: Reality is, Hope and Despair lie in the same places. And they're just a matter of perspective. What changed my perspective, was her.
Author: Richie Singh

Quote: The foundation stones for a balanced success are honesty, character, integrity, faith, love and loyalty.
Author: Zig Ziglar

Quote: You could rattle the stars, she whispered. You could do anything, if only you dared. And deep down, you know it, too. That's what scares you most.
Author: Sarah J. Maas

Quote: We had a blast at my magical birthday party. There were midgets, fairies, glass slippers, and I actually got to ride in a pumpkin.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Because you are the superhero fledgling. I'm just your more attractive sidekick. Oh, and the herd of nerds are your dorky minions.
Author: P.C. Cast

Quote: Making love to me is amazing. Wait, I meant: making love, to me, is amazing. The absence of two little commas nearly transformed me into a sex god.
Author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo
Origin: Love Quotes for the Ages. Specifically Ages 19-91.

Quote: We'd met at a carefree time, a moment full of promise, in its place now were the harsh lessons of the real world.
Author: Nicholas Sparks

Quote: Set patterns, incapable of adaptability, of pliability, only offer a better cage. Truth is outside of all patterns.
Author: Bruce Lee

Quote: A good novel tells us the truth about its hero; but a bad novel tells us the truth about its author.
Author: G.K. Chesterton

Quote: I just started working the graveyard shift at the cemetery. Come to think of it, every shift at the cemetery is the graveyard shift.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: And he took her in his arms and kissed her under the sunlit sky, and he cared not that they stood high upon the walls in the sight of many.
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien

Quote: I have no idea what's awaiting me, or what will happen when this all ends. For the moment I know this: there are sick people and they need curing.
Author: Albert Camus

Quote: Your kids require you most of all to love them for who they are, not to spend your whole time trying to correct them.
Author: Bill Ayers

Quote: I don't have a caustic sense of humor. What I find funny, that humor comes from a much gentler place.
Author: Vera Farmiga

Quote: Echo of your thoughts are more important than your actions because that has greater impact on the world.
Author: Amit Ray

Quote: You're paved in my heart like an old road. Like the pebbles in a pebble field, dirt in dirt, dust in dust, cobwebs in cobwebs.
Author: Kyung-sook Shin

Quote: Once war becomes a clash of absolutes, there is no breathing room for mercy. Absolute truth is blind truth.
Author: Deepak Chopra
Origin: The Third Jesus: The Christ We Cannot Ignore

Quote: True faith rests upon the character of God and asks no further proof than the moral perfections of the One who cannot lie.
Author: A.W. Tozer

Quote: If you want a love message to be heard, it has got to be sent out. To keep a lamp burning, we have to keep putting oil in it.
Author: Mother Teresa

Quote: Fear of God is a beginning of knowledge. The fear lessened on a higher knowledge. Walk with God is on the top of knowledge.
Author: Toba Beta
Origin: My Ancestor Was an Ancient Astronaut

Quote: Everyone must dream. We dream to give ourselves hope. To stop dreaming - well, that's like saying you can never change your fate. Isn't that true?
Author: Amy Tan

Quote: To see a World in a Grain of Sand And a Heaven in a Wild Flower, Hold Infinity in the palm of your hand And Eternity in an hour.
Author: William Blake

Quote: It's one thing to think you're worthless, and quite another for somebody else to tell you that you are.
Author: Jennifer Echols

Quote: All any of us wanted, really, was to know that we counted. That someone else's life would not have been as rich without us here.
Author: Jodi Picoult

Quote: I really don't even know you, and yet, in my life, you are forever entangled; to my history, inextricably bound.
Author: Sarah Ockler

Quote: Why not seize the pleasure at once? -- How often is happiness destroyed by preparation, foolish preparation!
Author: Jane Austen

Quote: We had some good times at school. I didn't know how good those times was till I left, but I guess that's the way of it
Author: Ron Rash

Quote: Great art is the outward expression of an inner life in the artist, and this inner life will result in his personal vision of the world.
Author: Edward Hopper

Quote: Science, my boy, is made up of mistakes, but they are mistakes which it is useful to make, because they lead little by little to the truth.
Author: Jules Verne
Origin: Journey to the Center of the Earth

Quote: Hope is some extraordinary spiritual grace that God gives us to control our fears, not to oust them.
Author: Vincent Joseph McNabb

Quote: It's not unpatriotic to denounce an injustice committed on our behalf, perhaps it's the most patriotic thing we can do.
Author: E.A. Bucchianeri

Quote: Poetry should be great and unobtrusive, a thing which enters into one's soul, and does not startle it or amaze it with itself, but with its subject.
Author: John Keats

Quote: She did not believe he could have really gone, because for her, to leave the person you loved was impossible.
Author: Jodi Lynn Anderson

Quote: He believed in God even if he was doubtful of men's claims to know God's mind. But that a God unable to forgive was no God at all.
Author: Cormac McCarthy

Quote: I can tell you I love you as many times as you can stand to hear it, but all it does is remind us that love is not enough. Not even close.
Author: Mark Andrus

Quote: We are not held back by the love we didn't receive in the past, but by the love we're not extending in the present.
Author: Marianne Williamson

Quote: There is not enough love and goodness in the world to permit giving any of it away to imaginary beings.
Author: Friedrich Nietzsche

Quote: Faith is like radar that sees through the fog -- the reality of things at a distance that the human eye cannot see.
Author: Corrie ten Boom

Quote: Faith is a marvel, and yet no human being is excluded from it; for that in which all human life is united is passion, and faith is a passion.
Author: Søren Kierkegaard

Quote: You stay safe, You love. You survive. You laugh and cry and struggle and sometimes you fail and sometimes you succeed. You Push.
Author: Carrie Ryan

Quote: Maybe I'm strange and perverse, but I've always thought there was something sexy about a compelling argument.
Author: Therese Doucet

Quote: Robert Walker as Bruno was excellent. He had elegance and humor, and the proper fondness for his mother.
Author: Patricia Highsmith

Quote: I love you, not just for now, but for always, and I dream of the day that you'll take me in your arms again.
Author: Nicholas Sparks

Quote: Lying in bed would be an altogether perfect and supreme experience if only one had a colored pencil long enough to draw on the ceiling.
Author: Gilbert K. Chesterton

Quote: The greatest discovery of any generation is that a human can alter his life by altering his attitude.
Author: William James

Quote: The whole art of teaching is only the art of awakening the natural curiosity of young minds for the purpose of satisfying it afterwards.
Author: Anatole France

Quote: Burdens of life become lighter when your heart is filled with love and we learn to forgive and forget with profound kindness.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: Gossip is just a tool to distract people who have nothing better to do from feeling jealous of those few of us still remaining with noble hearts.
Author: Anna Godbersen

Quote: Don't wait for other people to be loving, giving, compassionate, grateful, forgiving, generous, or friendly... lead the way!
Author: Steve Maraboli
Origin: Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

Quote: Every immigrant who comes here should be required within five years to learn English or leave the country.
Author: Theodore Roosevelt

Quote: If someone is genuine, compassionate and kind, that is a turn-on. A sense of humor is a huge turn on, and that goes hand in hand with intelligence.
Author: Jennifer Nettles

Quote: Success is like death. The more successful you become, the higher the houses in the hills get and the higher the fences get.
Author: Kevin Spacey

Quote: Glory is a heavy burden, a murdering poison, and to bear it is an art. And to have that art is rare.
Author: Oriana Fallaci

Quote: Prayer [is] the quiet, persistent living of our life of desire and faith in the presence of our God.
Author: Andrew Murray
Origin: With Christ in the School of Prayer

Quote: God is the supreme uncreated light of which Wisdom is born, but there was never a time when God's Wisdom did not exist.
Author: Merritt Y. Hughes

Quote: WORTH IT and perfect are different things. No one's perfect, yet in romance, everyone becomes WORTH IT. And that's the trick.
Author: Maureen Johnson

Quote: The whole of the world could be deduced from the smallest grain of sand if one studied it closely enough.
Author: Christopher Paolini

Quote: I believe in a higher consciousness. I also believe that nature is supremely conscious. A tree is more conscious than we are.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: Darling, when things go wrong in life, you lift your chin, put on a ravishing smile, mix yourself a little cocktail...
Author: Sophie Kinsella

Quote: While formal schooling is an important advantage, it is not a guarantee of success nor is its absence a fatal handicap.
Author: Ray Kroc

Quote: It's a metaphor, see: You put the killing thing right between your teeth, but you don't give it the power to do its killing.
Author: John Green

Quote: A person learns how to love himself through the simple acts of loving and being loved by someone else.
Author: Haruki Murakami

Quote: You have to take risks. We will only understand the miracle of life fully when we allow the unexpected to happen.
Author: Paulo Coelho

Quote: Curious that we spend more time congratulating people who have succeeded than encouraging people who have not.
Author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

Quote: Formal education and current position can define your worthiness. What makes you extraordinary is defined by your attitude towards others.
Author: Ashish Patel

Quote: Everything human is pathetic. The secret source of humor itself is not joy but sorrow. There is no humor in heaven.
Author: Mark Twain

Quote: For thousands of nights I dreamed of making love to you. No man on earth has ever hated sunrise as I do.
Author: Lisa Kleypas

Quote: Our virtues and our failings are inseparable, like force and matter. When they separate, man is no more.
Author: Nikola Telsa
Origin: The Problem of Increasing Human Energy

Quote: The best teacher is he who kindles the fire of imaginations and does not fill minds with only information.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: There's no better way to inform and expand you mind on a regular basis than to get into the habit of reading good literature.
Author: Stephen R. Covey
Origin: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People: Powerful Lessons in Personal Change

Quote: I can only imagine how happy life would be if we could stay so grounded in our faith that we would never waver in our positive attitudes.
Author: Tracie Miles
Origin: Stressed- Less Living: Finding God's Peace in Your Chaotic World

Quote: Love is like quicksilver in the hand. Leave the fingers open and it stays. Clutch it and it darts away.
Author: Dorothy Parker

Quote: Knowledge is like money: To be of value it must circulate, and in circulating it can increase in quantity and, hopefully, in value.
Author: Louis L'Amour

Quote: I am a poor man and of little worth, who is laboring in that art that God has given me in order to extend my life as long as possible.
Author: Michelangelo

Quote: He was very religious; he believed that he had a secret pact with God which exempted him from doing good in exchange for prayers and piety.
Author: Jorge Luis Borges

Quote: One rarely falls in love without being as much attracted to what is interestingly wrong with someone as what is objectively healthy.
Author: Alain de Botton

Quote: The heart sees what the eyes do not, and the heart hears what the ears cannot. Sight and sound are vibrations, and so is love.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: What we are waiting for is not as important as what happens to us while we are waiting. Trust the process.
Author: Mandy Hale
Origin: The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

Quote: One of the greatest tragedies in life is to lose your own sense of self and accept the version of you that is expected by everyone else.
Author: K.L. Toth

Quote: There is only one way to happiness and that is to cease worrying about things which are beyond the power or our will.
Author: Epictetus

Quote: The snake which cannot cast its skin has to die. As well the minds which are prevented from changing their opinions; they cease to be mind.
Author: Friedrich Nietzsche

Quote: We have to rise above bad fortune. We have to be in the good and enjoy the good, study and work and adventure and friendship and community and love.
Author: Joshua Prager

Quote: If a believer demands that I, as a nonbeliever, observe his taboos in the public domain, he is not asking for my respect, but for my submission.
Author: Flemming Rose

Quote: I have been very blessed in my life and rewarded with good friends and good health. I am grateful and happy to be able to share this.
Author: Eric Idle

Quote: As every divided kingdom falls, so every mind divided between many studies confounds and saps itself.
Author: Leonardo da Vinci

Quote: I am coming to terms with the fact that loving someone requires a leap of faith, and that a soft landing is never guaranteed.
Author: Sarah Dessen

Quote: I love letting her know how much she means to me. A love in secret is but a shadow that's cold and lonely.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: In trying to justify the humanities, as in trying to live a life, what may turn out to matter most is holding one's nerve.
Author: Stefan Collini

Quote: Standing there, staring at the long shelves crammed with books, I felt myself relax and was suddenly at peace.
Author: Helene Hanff

Quote: But as long as something is never even started, you never have to worry about it ending. It has endless potential.
Author: Sarah Dessen

Quote: Play around. Dive into absurdity and write. Take chances. You will succeed if you are fearless of failure.
Author: Natalie Goldberg
Origin: Writing Down the Bones: Freeing the Writer Within

Quote: After all, life hasn't much to offer except youth, and I suppose for older people, the love of youth in others.
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

Quote: Despair is typical of those who do not understand the causes of evil, see no way out, and are incapable of struggle.
Author: Vladimir Lenin

Quote: It had never occurred to me that our lives, which had been so closely interwoven, could unravel with such speed.
Author: Kazuo Ishiguro

Quote: To me, the perfect date consists of dinner, dancing, and sex with a girl who has no stomach or legs, but does have an overactive sex drive.
Author: Jarod Kintz

Quote: Obama's endorsement of gay marriage is hardly as consequential as Johnson's legislative success on civil rights.
Author: Robert Dallek

Quote: I would love to make my music and be completely anonymous, but that doesn't work. You can't have success and be faceless.
Author: Sarah McLachlan

Quote: I simply can't build my hopes on a foundation of confusion, misery and death... I think... peace and tranquillity will return again.
Author: Anne Frank

Quote: Remember, remember, this is now, and now, and now. Live it, feel it, cling to it. I want to become acutely aware of all I've taken for granted.
Author: Sylvia Plath

Quote: Finding good partners is the key to success in anything: in business, in marriage and, especially, in investing.
Author: Robert Kiyosaki

Quote: Only the person who has experienced light and darkness, war and peace, rise and fall, only that person has truly experienced life.
Author: Stefan Zweig

Quote: I have never killed anybody, it is true, but it is because I lacked the courage or the time, not because I lacked the desire
Author: Eduardo Galeano

Quote: I love puns. I've been known to turn the car around just to take advantage of a good pun situation. It really is the highest form of humor.
Author: Karin Slaughter

Quote: We made love like a goat has four legs like a table. If your dining room table can walk, it's best to eat while sitting in wheelchairs.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: The question is not how much work are you willing to put in, but what are you willing to take out? Success is sacrifice.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: If you violate laws of God, you're a sinner. If you violate laws of men, you're a criminal. If you violate your own laws, you're pathetic.
Author: Toba Beta

Quote: Tourism, human circulation considered as consumption is fundamentally nothing more than the leisure of going to see what has become banal.
Author: Guy Debord

Quote: That must be love: when everything else in the world could implode and you wouldn't care as long as you had that one person standing beside you.
Author: Katie McGarry

Quote: God does not patch up the old life, or make certain repairs on the old life; He gives a new life, through the new birth.
Author: Kathryn Kuhlman

Quote: It is one of the beautiful compensations of life that no man can sincerely try to help another without helping himself.
Author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Quote: No matter how much you know, your destiny depends on the Wisdom, Knowledge and Understanding you display.
Author: James C. Uwandu

Quote: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
Author: G.K. Chesterton

Quote: America will never be destroyed from the outside. If we falter and lose our freedoms, it will be because we destroyed ourselves.
Author: Abraham Lincoln

Quote: The more I learn about the universe, the less convinced I am that there's any sort of benevolent force that has anything to do with it, at all.
Author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

Quote: I may err but I am not a heretic, for the first has to do with the mind and the second with the will!
Author: Meister Eckhart

Quote: Nobody really cares if you're miserable, so you might as well be happy, and make the most of where you are
Author: Germany Kent

Quote: Question the answers, I repeated every class. Reevaluate your conclusions when the evidence changes.
Author: Craig M. Mullaney
Origin: The Unforgiving Minute: A Soldier's Education

Quote: Judged by the normal standards of human affairs, the lives of men and women of God may look overburdened with suffering, and even inconclusive.
Author: Eknath Easwaran
Origin: Nonviolent Soldier of Islam: Badshah Khan: A Man to Match His Mountains

Quote: In life, there is always that special person who shapes who you are, who helps to determine the person you become.
Author: Molly Ringwald

Quote: For someone who is supposed to be a knight in shining armor, you're acting like an ass in aluminum foil.
Author: Lisa Logue

Quote: I can do pain. That's the easy part of life. It's everything else, happiness, laughter, love, that's fucking complicated.
Author: Jessica Sorensen

Quote: Children know perfectly well that unicorns aren't real, but they also know that books about unicorns, if they are good books, are true books.
Author: Ursula K. Le Guin

Quote: The right to search for the truth implies also a duty; one must not conceal any part of what one has recognized to be the truth.
Author: Albert Einstein

Quote: When was the last time you read a book? The truth now. And picture books don't count- I mean something with print in it.
Author: William Goldman

Quote: A good plan violently executed now is better than a perfect plan executed at some indefinite time in the future.
Author: George S. Patton Jr.

Quote: We'd stared into the face of Death, and Death blinked first. You'd think that would make us feel brave and invincible. It didn't.
Author: Rick Yancey

Quote: Is your Happy Meal full of dancing? Put a box around it and sell it to kids. They could use the exercise.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Oh the beauty of nature! Oh the magical heart touching flower. My heart wants to bloom like you with love, joy, and laughter.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: You are the sum of what you believe. Your capabilities are limitless when you allow yourself to be so.
Author: Steven Cuoco

Quote: Wasn't hitting bottom the thing you had to do to knock some sense into yourself? Wasn't hitting bottom the thing that showed you which way was up?
Author: Rainbow Rowell

Quote: To judge from the notions expounded by theologians, one must conclude that God created most men simply with a view to crowding hell.
Author: Marquis de Sade

Quote: To all my librarian friends, champions of books, true magicians in the House of Life. Without you, this writer would be lost in the Dust.
Author: Rick Riordan

Quote: Certain thoughts are prayers. There are moments when, whatever be the attitude of the body, the soul is on its knees.
Author: Victor Hugo

Quote: If you think life is magical or life is hard, either way you are right. Your thoughts are the source of reality.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: Bring love and peace and happiness and beautiful lives into the world in my honor. Thank you. Love you.
Author: Aron Ralston

Quote: Compromise, if not the spice of life, is its solidity. It is what makes nations great and marriages happy
Author: Phyllis McGinley

Quote: A man who has come to the full realization of his responsibility to men won't stick to one man. It will be foolhardy if he does that.
Author: Ogwo David Emenike

Quote: Because this is what happens when you try to run from the past. It just doesn't catch up, it overtakes ... blotting out the future.
Author: Sarah Dessen

Quote: The choices you make now, the people you surround yourself with, they all have the potential to affect your life, even who you are, forever.
Author: Sarah Dessen

Quote: And I remembered something else that makes us human: faith, the only weapon in our arsenal to battle doubt.
Author: Jodi Picoult

Quote: So what? You're another person, so of course you look different. What do you need to be ashamed for?
Author: Yana Toboso

Quote: There are all kinds of ways for a relationship to be tested, even broken, some, irrevocably; it's the endings we're unprepared for.
Author: Katherine Owen

Quote: What was it about not knowing a person that allowed you to wonder whether she might be the answer to all your problems?
Author: Keith Ablow

Quote: A great book should leave you with many experiences, and slightly exhausted at the end. You live several lives while reading.
Author: William Styron

Quote: There are three things all wise men fear: the sea in storm, a night with no moon, and the anger of a gentle man.
Author: Patrick Rothfuss

Quote: The pink sunset reminds me of her, and the calm water matches the calm that images of her bring to my heart.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: The resistance that you fight physically in the gym and the resistance that you fight in life can only build a strong character.
Author: Arnold Schwarzenegger

Quote: All that mankind has done, thought, gained, or been; it is lying as in magic preservation in the pages of books.
Author: Thomas Carlyle

Quote: Love falls in love with love; comes like an echo sounding back, searches its mirrored shadow within a look.
Author: Mocco Wollert

Quote: Don't waste your time hating a failure. Failure is a greater teacher than success. Listen, learn, go on.
Author: Clarissa Pinkola Estés
Origin: Women Who Run With the Wolves: Myths and Stories of the Wild Woman Archetype

Quote: But hurry, let's entwine ourselves as one, our mouth broken, our soul bitten by love, so time discovers us safely destroyed.
Author: Federico García Lorca

Quote: A tree with red leaves is like an old man with gray hair. Likewise, my love for you was blue, but now it's orange, and that's a compliment.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Isn't it strange that I know you'd risk your life to save mine, but I don't even know what your favorite color is?
Author: Suzanne Collins

Quote: In the practical art of war, the best thing of all is to take the enemy's country whole and intact; to shatter and destroy it is not so good.
Author: Sun Tzu

Quote: He'd always been a man who followed his head and not his heart. The heart was just a bloody motor. The head was meant to drive
Author: Mario Puzo

Quote: My greatest desire is that the hope that has overcome fear in my country will help vanquish it around the world.
Author: Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva

Quote: Deliverance is not for me in renunciation. I feel the embrace of freedom in a thousand bonds of delight.
Author: Rabindranath Tagore

Quote: You Stole my loneliness. I may have given you wings, but you've become my gravity. I'll never be free of your force.
Author: Pepper Winters

Quote: My father once told me that it's not enough for a man to be lucky; that a guy has to know when that streak is on for him.
Author: Henry Mosquera

Quote: The lungs are fishing nets for smoke, and I inhaled her essence like she was marijuana grown just for me.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: My wife, my daughters, even my grandchildren are funny. You've got to keep a sense of humor because anger destroys you.
Author: Michael Caine

Quote: Don't step backward toward nothing, step forward toward something, better awkward steps forward, than coward steps backward.
Author: Victoria Addino

Quote: Competition is healthy. Especially when all your competitors are unhealthy, and hopefully sick and absent during the competition.
Author: Jarod Kintz

Quote: Once we believe in ourselves, we can risk curiosity, wonder, spontaneous delight, or any experience that reveals the human spirit.
Author: e. e. cummings

Quote: Amid the thousands of shrill voices screaming for our attention, there is but on Voice we need to hear. The voice of the Lord Jesus Christ.
Author: David Jeremiah
Origin: Until Christ Returns: Living Faithfully Today While We Wait for Our Glorious Tomorrow

Quote: I think the notion of retirement is just a dreadful, dreadful idea and I hope I never have to do that.
Author: Michael Moritz

Quote: There's no trouble in this world so serious that it can't be cured with a hot bath, a glass of whiskey, and the Book of Common Prayer.
Author: Elizabeth Gilbert

Quote: Honey, nobody ever knows someone completely. You have to just enjoy what you do know and have faith in the rest.
Author: Erin McCarthy

Quote: What the society thinks is of no interest to me. All that's important is how I see myself. I know who I am. I know the value of my work.
Author: Robin S. Sharma
Origin: The Leader Who Had No Title: A Modern Fable on Real Success in Business and in

Quote: Being able to write becomes a kind of shield, a way of hiding, a way of too instantly transforming pain into honey.
Author: John Updike

Quote: Most institutions demand unqualified faith; but the institution of science makes skepticism a virtue.
Author: Robert K. Merton

Quote: Someone needs to fight, someone needs to sacrifice, someone needs to inspire, someone needs to be a hero.
Author: Amit Kalantri

Quote: If you don't receive love from the ones who are meant to love you, you will never stop looking for it.
Author: Robert Goolrick
Origin: The End of the World as We Know It: Scenes from a Life

Quote: I love the stars. Because they can't say anything. I love the stars. Because they do not judge anyone.
Author: Natsuki Takaya

Quote: A library outranks any other one thing a community can do to benefit its people. It is a never failing spring in the desert.
Author: Andrew Carnegie

Quote: One in four people have a mental illness. You can be the one to make it two in four, if you'll join me for a dance.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: It's a truth I love you, It's a hope you do, And may I live and die with it, Not knowing that you don't.
Author: Amit Abraham

Quote: Happiness is a butterfly, which when pursued, is always just beyond your grasp, but which, if you will sit down quietly, may alight upon you.
Author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

Quote: Art is the child of Nature; yes, her darling child, in whom we trace the features of the mother's face, her aspect and her attitude.
Author: Beck

Quote: The action of a soulful person covertly makes many people brave and bold without the need for them to be advised or told.
Author: Anuj Somany

Quote: Leave me with my leftover meatloaf and my Yesterday Sandwich. I'll be in love tomorrow, if you come back with the ketchup.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: Love quotes for the ages. And the ageless sages.

Quote: I liked you, cop. From the moment I met you. No... not the first moment. I wanted to kill you when I first met you. But then I liked you. A lot.
Author: J.R. Ward

Quote: My past is black and my future is blank. My only canvas is the present. The best color to paint with is love.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Where would we have been without the discoveries of our predecessors, and where are we going without your discoveries?
Author: Ogwo David Emenike

Quote: I'm not sure if there's one right place I'm supposed to be, he said, but I know a couple of wrong places I'd give a second try in a heartbeat.
Author: Brian Andreas

Quote: Choose to long for love, to live in love, and to be the love so that you can achieve higher consciousness.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: Valuing knowledge above all else results in a lust for power, and that leads men into dark and empty places.
Author: Veronica Roth

Quote: When a poet digs himself into a hole, he doesn't climb out. He digs deeper, enjoys the scenery, and comes out the other side enlightened.
Author: Criss Jami

Quote: And as I watched him, I knew that in every dark night there was, somewhere, a small light burning that could never be quenched.
Author: Juliet Marillier

Quote: There is no scientific answer for success. You can't define it. You've simply got to live it and do it.
Author: Anita Roddick

Quote: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, but do so with all your heart.
Author: Marcus Aurelius

Quote: Choice forms the divider which is responsible for the formation of all futures that can be possible.
Author: Stephen Richards
Origin: Think Your way to Success: Let Your Dreams Run Free

Quote: Imagination was given to man to compensate him for what he is not; a sense of humor to console him for what he is.
Author: Francis Bacon

Quote: I've learned that you know your husband still loves you when there are two brownies left and he takes the smaller one.
Author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

Quote: Things get done only if the data we gather can inform and inspire those in a position to make difference.
Author: Mike Schmoker

Quote: I don't wait for moods. You accomplish nothing if you do that. Your mind must know it has got to get down to work.
Author: Pearl S. Buck

Quote: You have to appreciate where you have come from to know who you are in the present and whom you would like to be in the future.
Author: Truth Devour

Quote: This time the fluttery feeling in my stomach was more intense. It made the inside of my thighs tingle and my breathing deepen.
Author: P.C. Cast

Quote: There are a thousand thoughts lying within a man that he does not know till he takes up the pen to write.
Author: William Makepeace Thackeray
Origin: The History of Henry Esmond, Esq.

Quote: A compromise is the art of dividing a cake in such a way that everyone believes he has the biggest piece.
Author: Ludwig Erhard

Quote: Until this moment, I had not realized that someone could break your heart twice, along the very same fault lines.
Author: Jodi Picoult

Quote: There are only two lasting bequests we can hope to give our children. One of these is roots, the other, wings.
Author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

Quote: The greatest reward for a man's failure is not necessarily the consequent success, but what he becomes by it.
Author: Ogwo David Emenike
Origin: The Fortune in Failing: Decoding the Message of Failure

Quote: Nothing travels faster than the speed of light, with the possible exception of bad news, which obeys its own special laws.
Author: Douglas Adams

Quote: One of the deep secrets of life is that all that is really worth the doing is what we do for others.
Author: Lewis Carroll

Quote: This universe belongs to those who know how to see, feel, and love the beauty of life and the universe.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: How you spend your time is more important than how you spend your money. Money mistakes can be corrected, but time is gone forever.
Author: David Norris

Quote: The first rule about the low hanging fruit principle is to always watch out for low hanging branches, they're the ones to take it away from you.
Author: Stephen Richards

Quote: I receive Thee ransom of my soul. For love of Thee have I studied and kept vigil toiled preached and taught...
Author: Thomas Aquinas

Quote: I like the immaterial world. I like to live among thoughts and images of the past and the possible, and even of the impossible, now and then.
Author: Thomas Love Peacock

Quote: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul - and sings the tunes without the words - and never stops at all.
Author: Emily Dickinson

Quote: There are two things that men should never weary of, goodness and humility; we get none too much of them in this rough world among cold, proud people.
Author: Robert Louis Stevenson

Quote: It's so much easier to convince yourself you're madly in love with someone when you know nothing about him.
Author: Megan McCafferty

Quote: I like a cook who smiles out loud when he tastes his own work. Let God worry about your modesty; I want to see your enthusiasm.
Author: Robert Farrar Capon

Quote: Affliction is often that thing which prepares an ordinary person for some sort of an extraordinary destiny.
Author: C.S. Lewis

Quote: I feel I am lucky. I am grateful for this life that God has given me. I am happy, as I am getting to do work that I want to do and enjoy doing it.
Author: Sonam Kapoor

Quote: I don't mind waiting rooms. I'm waiting on the love of my life, so I may as well have a seat, right?
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: Whenever You're Gone, I'm Here For You

Quote: When I like people immensely I never tell their names to anyone. It is like surrendering a part of them. I have grown to love secrecy.
Author: Oscar Wilde

Quote: Do what you do only for God's sake, start for God's sake, work for God's sake, and act within the sphere of God's good approval.
Author: Said Nursî

Quote: Success goes to the ones who do. Get up. Show up. Throw up if you have to. Do it afraid, but do it no matter.
Author: Toni Sorenson

Quote: Until women themselves reject stigma and refuse to feel shame for the way others treat them, they have no hope of achieving full human stature.
Author: Germaine Greer

Quote: Love is a springtime plant that perfumes everything with its hope, even the ruins to which it clings.
Author: Gustave Flaubert

Quote: For a successful technology, reality must take precedence over public relations, for nature cannot be fooled.
Author: Richard Feynman

Quote: .. she believed a great happiness awaited her somewhere, and for this reason she remained calm as the days flew by.
Author: Gyula Krúdy

Quote: I had parents who instilled in me the importance of love, morals and hard work. I give God all the glory because he has brought me through so much.
Author: Junior Seau

Quote: Mary had a little lamb, its fleece electrostatic / And everywhere Mary went, the lights became erratic.
Author: David Foster Wallace

Quote: I am simply of the opinion that you cannot be taught to write. You have to spend a lifetime in love with words.
Author: Craig Claiborne

Quote: The more you take responsibility for your attitudes, the more you can adjust yourself in a forward thinking manner.
Author: Deborah Day

Quote: When it's raining, instead of using an umbrella, try holding an empty drinking glass above your head. That's exactly how I thirst for your love.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: It's not what you say out of your mouth that determines your life, it's what you whisper to yourself that has the most power!
Author: Robert T. Kiyosaki

Quote: The greatest happiness of life is the conviction that we are loved -- loved for ourselves, or rather, loved in spite of ourselves.
Author: Victor Hugo

Quote: Some people don't realize what they have until it's gone, but that does not always mean they are supposed to get it back.
Author: Stephan Labossiere

Quote: Reading about the response of people in stories, plays, poems, helps us to respond more courageously and openly at our own moments of turning.
Author: Madeleine L'Engle
Origin: Walking on Water: Reflections on Faith and Art

Quote: I just invented a hug machine. It's solar powered so you can use it when you feel depressed, like on a cloudy day. Shit.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: The price we have paid for expecting to be so much more than our ancestors is a perpetual anxiety that we are far from being all we might be.
Author: Alain de Botton

Quote: The ability to dream is all I have to give. That is my responsibility; that is my burden. And even I grow tired.
Author: Harlan Ellison

Quote: I've been absolutely terrified every moment of my life - and I've never let it keep me from doing a single thing I wanted to do.
Author: Georgia O'Keeffe

Quote: I have gained this by philosophy ... I do without being ordered what some are constrained to do by their fear of the law.
Author: Aristotle

Quote: We all didn't come into to the world at the same time so it makes sense that we don't leave it at the same time.
Author: Lurlene McDaniel

Quote: Modern art is what happens when painters stop looking at girls and persuade themselves that they have a better idea.
Author: John Ciardi

Quote: Why didn't I learn to treat everything like it was the last time. My greatest regret was how much I believed in the future.
Author: Jonathan Safran Foer

Quote: Be of good cheer about death, and know this of a truth, that no evil can happen to a good man, either in life or after death.
Author: Socrates

Quote: Success depends upon previous preparation, and without such preparation there is sure to be failure.
Author: Confucius

Quote: Prior to Wordsworth, humor was an essential part of poetry. I mean, they don't call them Shakespeare comedies for nothing.
Author: William Collins

Quote: Awareness is above intellect. Intellect is above speech. Speech is above knowledge. For knowledge in itself is just a name.
Author: Gian Kumar

Quote: Love is a tomato. And while it's true that I can live without a tomato, I could sure go for some ketchup.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: The real test of friendship is: can you literally do nothing with the other person? Can you enjoy those moments of life that are utterly simple?
Author: Eugene Kennedy

Quote: Musical training is a more potent instrument than any other, because rhythm and harmony find their way into the inward places of the soul.
Author: Plato

Quote: I've only been in love one time, I said as I held up my pinky finger. I could have held up my index finger, but I wasn't in love that long.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: If you would be a real seeker after truth, it is necessary that at least once in your life you doubt, as far as possible, all things.
Author: Rene Descartes

Quote: There is no happiness like that of being loved by your fellow creatures, and feeling that your presence is an addition to their comfort.
Author: Charlotte Brontë

Quote: For a moment I was distracted. Books always did that to me... I liked the creamy pages, the smell of ink, all the secrets locked inside.
Author: Elizabeth C. Bunce

Quote: Why do you so earnestly seek the truth in distant places? Look for delusion and truth in the bottom of your own heart.
Author: Ryokan

Quote: The tears of the world are a constant quantity. For each one who begins to weep somewhere else another stops. The same is true of the laugh.
Author: Samuel Beckett

Quote: Always be open to inspiration. You never know where it may come from. Begin with an open mind, end with an inspired heart.
Author: Sheri Fink

Quote: Some people think football [soccer] is a matter of life and death. I don't like that attitude. I can assure them it is much more serious than that.
Author: Bill Shankly

Quote: Witness also that when we talk about literature, we do so in the present tense. When we speak of the dead, we are not so kind.
Author: John Green

Quote: Enjoying success requires the ability to adapt. Only by being open to change will you have a true opportunity to get the most from your talent.
Author: Nolan Ryan

Quote: Definitions are the guardians of rationality, the first line of defense against the chaos of mental disintegration.
Author: Ayn Rand

Quote: I die, as I have lived, a free spirit, an Anarchist, owing no allegiance to rulers, heavenly or earthly.
Author: Voltairine de Cleyre

Quote: I grew a garden in her heart. There was plenty of space for flowers, because her heart was completely empty of love.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Celebrating faith over reason is merely a way of denying what is, in favor of embracing any whim that strikes your fancy.
Author: Terry Goodkind

Quote: A religion that takes no account of practical affairs and does not help to solve them is no religion.
Author: Mahatma Gandhi

Quote: I want to continue being crazy; living my life the way I dream it, and not the way the other people want it to be.
Author: Paulo Coelho

Quote: When I look at my old pictures, all I can see is what I used to be but am no longer. I think: What I can see is what I am not.
Author: Aleksandar Hemon

Quote: You may be born thousands of years from now, but over the boundaries of time I am sending you my love and wishing you a joyful and wonderful life.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: The pages are still blank, but there is a miraculous feeling of the words being there, written in invisible ink and clamoring to become visible
Author: Vladimir Nabokov

Quote: Here's all you have to know about men and women: women are crazy, men are stupid. And the main reason women are crazy is that men are stupid.
Author: George Carlin
Origin: When Will Jesus Bring The Pork Chops?

Quote: Kindness in words creates confidence. Kindness in thinking creates profoundness. Kindness in giving creates love.
Author: Lao Tzu

Quote: Every night I sleep like a log. In a lit fireplace. If you bring the marshmallows, I'll bring the burning desire for you.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: This time when we kiss, I feel it in the pit of my stomach, I feel it in my heart. And I realize love isn't about sex. It's about connection.
Author: Ellen Hopkins

Quote: So my humor, I'd say, comes from a mixture of lowbrow comedy shows and highbrow theater. It's an interesting mix.
Author: Jasper Fforde

Quote: What a wee little part of a person's life are his acts and his words! His real life is led in his head, and is known to none but himself.
Author: Mark Twain

Quote: The quality of the relationships that students have in class with their peers and teachers is important to their success in school.
Author: Bob Pletka
Origin: Educating the Net Generation: How to Engage Students in the 21st Century

Quote: It's strange how dreams get under your skin and give your heart a test for what's real and what's imaginary.
Author: Jason Mraz

Quote: God's love for his people is so great that it turns God against himself, his love against his justice.
Author: Pope Benedict XVI
Origin: God Is Love: Deus Caritas Est

Quote: Boring damned people. All over the earth. Propagating more boring damned people. What a horror show. The earth swarmed with them.
Author: Charles Bukowski

Quote: I see a light in the kitchen. Let us not deprive Molly any longer of the chance to deplore how thin you are.
Author: J.K. Rowling
Origin: Harry Potter and the Half- Blood Prince

Quote: Faith is not about finding meaning in the world, there may be no such thing -- faith is the belief in our capacity to create meaningful lives.
Author: Terry Tempest Williams

Quote: But what really matters is not what you believe but the faith and conviction with which you believe...
Author: Knut Hamsun

Quote: Sometimes you're not ready to give the world quite what it wants. And that's okay, because the Earth is generously patient.
Author: Jaree Francis

Quote: The lovers of the world are the ones who matter. Forget the rest. Jesus was a lover. He doesn't hate anyone.
Author: Scylar Tyberius

Quote: I would never let anyone else borrow my heart if I know there's a chance in hell you might want it back.
Author: Colleen Hoover

Quote: In every moment we are faced with choices that will serve our highest intentions, or gratify our unconscious desires.
Author: Dashama Konah Gordon
Origin: Journey to Joyful: Transform Your Life with Pranashama Yoga

Quote: Pure Soul and circumstances; there are only these two; but the disturbance of egoism does not allow it to be at peace.
Author: Dada Bhagwan

Quote: I like to have a martini, Two at the very most. After three I'm under the table, after four I'm under my host.
Author: Dorothy Parker

Quote: We need to walk to know sacred places, those around us and those within. We need to walk to remember the songs.
Author: Joseph Bruchac

Quote: All I know is that the closer I get to God, the deeper I get into the Bible, and the heavier the burden seems on my shoulders.
Author: Tim LaHaye

Quote: There is no such thing as a problem without a gift for you in its hands. You seek problems because you need their gifts.
Author: Richard Bach

Quote: Every one suspects himself of at least one of the cardinal virtues, and this is mine: I am one of the few honest people that I have ever known.
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

Quote: Humor has always been important to me. If there is a shield of faith that you can keep up against difficulties, humor is the Teflon coating.
Author: Laurel Lea

Quote: I should combine a car bumper with a belt, for a fashion accessory that's safe for office romances. Powdered love poetry sold separately.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Teenagers who do not go to church are adored by God, but they don't get to meet some of the people who love God back.
Author: Anne Lamott
Origin: Plan B: Further Thoughts on Faith

Quote: All those who offer an opinion on any doubtful point should first clear their minds of every sentiment of dislike, friendship, anger or pity.
Author: Sallust

Quote: Do not let your difficulties fill you with anxiety, after all it is only in the darkest nights that stars shine more brightly.
Author: Hazrat Ali Ibn Abu- Talib A.S

Quote: There is no such things as magic, though there is such a thing as knowledge of the hidden ways of Nature.
Author: H. Rider Haggard

Quote: What is a man's life but a prelude to his death? And what is death but a long sleep, a most welcome forgetfulness.
Author: Lisa M. Klein

Quote: You'll never reach perfection because there's always room for improvement. Yet get along the way to perfection, you'll learn to get better.
Author: Hlovate

Quote: Microwavable ice cream is the future. Don't wait for love to melt all over your crotch. Scoop some Ice Cream Soup today.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: He - and if there is a God, I am convinced he is a he, because no woman could or would ever fuck things up this badly.
Author: George Carlin

Quote: Our lives are the only meaningful expression of what we believe and in Whom we believe. And the only real wealth, for any of us, lies in our faith.
Author: Gordon B. Hinckley

Quote: Music is the language of the universe. Music was there even before the concept of life. Music is one of the most powerful catalysts of evolution.
Author: Debasish Mridha

Quote: Each child's story is worthy of telling. There shouldn't be a sliding scale of death. The weight of it is crushing.
Author: Anderson Cooper
Origin: Dispatches from the Edge: A Memoir of War, Disasters, and Survival

Quote: It seems to me that anyone whose library consists of a Kindle lying on a table is some sort of bloodless nerd.
Author: Penelope Lively

Quote: After all, that was a main purpose of science: to make things of all kinds happen sooner than they otherwise would.
Author: Robert Aickman

Quote: Free running is wall dancing, and that's why I do my best moves while sitting in a rocking chair. I like to relax dangerously.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: The important thing in science is not so much to obtain new facts as to discover new ways of thinking about them
Author: William Lawrence Bragg

Quote: The world must be all fucked up, "he said then, "when men travel first class and literature goes as freight.
Author: Gabriel Garcí­a Márquez

Quote: Success in almost any field depends more on energy and drive than it does on intelligence. This explains why we have so many stupid leaders.
Author: Sloan Wilson

Quote: The most wonderful and the strongest things in the world, you know, are just the things which no one can see.
Author: Charles Kingsley

Quote: The greatest personal limitation is to be found not in the things you want to do and can't, but in the things you've never considered doing.
Author: Richard Bandler

Quote: It is true that those we meet can change us, sometimes so profoundly that we are not the same afterwards, even unto our names.
Author: Yann Martel

Quote: The reason birds can fly and we can't is simply because they have perfect faith, for to have faith is to have wings.
Author: J.M. Barrie

Quote: My stories are very somber, so I think I need the comic ingredient. Besides, life has so much humor.
Author: Manuel Puig

Quote: Whatever success I may have attained is due to the fact that since I was old enough to work at all, my ambition has never deserted me.
Author: Anna Held

Quote: It is unwise to be too sure of one's own wisdom. It is healthy to be reminded that the strongest might weaken and the wisest might err.
Author: Mahatma Gandhi

Quote: I hope our wisdom will grow with our power, and teach us, that the less we use our power the greater it will be.
Author: Thomas Jefferson

Quote: Do not live in the shadow of the masters for ever. Learn to live in the light of your soul. Life deserves full expression.
Author: Amit Ray

Quote: Nations, as well as men, almost always betray the most prominent features of their future destiny in their earliest years.
Author: Alexis de Tocqueville

Quote: Blake took her face in his hands. "You let me touch you. Kiss you. Your skin? It feels like piano keys. My hands know just where to go.
Author: Debra Anastasia

Quote: A poet's mission is to make words do more work than they normally do, to make them work on more than one level.
Author: Jay- Z

Quote: Why don't you think of [God] as the one who is coming, who has been approaching from all eternity... the ultimate fruit of a tree whose leaves we are.
Author: Rainer Maria Rilke

Quote: Nine out of ten politicians are thieves. The tenth politician is a liar, and the one who conducted the survey.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: It is very nearly impossible to become an educated person in a country so distrustful of the independent mind.
Author: James Baldwin

Quote: You said we all want there to be more than this! Well, there's always more than this. There's always something you don't know.
Author: Patrick Ness

Quote: It is of great importance, when we begin to practise prayer, not to let ourselves be frightened by our own thoughts.
Author: Teresa of Ávila
Origin: The Life of Saint Teresa of Ávila by Herself

Quote: All profound distraction opens certain doors. You have to allow yourself to be distracted when you are unable to concentrate.
Author: Julio Cortázar
Origin: Around the Day in Eighty Worlds

Quote: The conception of each star was at the point of no return; of a desperate soul struggling to master the winds!
Author: C. JoyBell C.

Quote: Had we but world enough and time, This coyness, lady, were no crime. We would sit down, and think which way To walk, and pass our long love's day.
Author: Andrew Marvell

Quote: My skull is soundproof. And thank God too, or else you'd be able to hear all the horrible things I'm thinking about you.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: At even one penny, this book would be overpriced. In fact, free is too expensive, because you'd still waste time by reading it.

Quote: There are two ways to reach me: by way of kisses or by way of the imagination. But there is a hierarchy: the kisses alone don't work.
Author: Anaïs Nin

Quote: I watch you like a mirror, because I see in you what I see in myself. Who we love is a reflection of who we are.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: Write like no one is reading 3

Quote: Hanson got to sleep with you, and I didn't," he said, his own jaw a little tight. "So I stole his car.
Author: Tara Janzen

Quote: Everyone is a salesman, and the product is each person. Personal branding is being conscious to the continual nature of selling yourself.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: The notion that a human being should be constantly happy is a uniquely modern, uniquely American, uniquely destructive idea.
Author: Andrew Weil

Quote: We at BMW do not build cars as consumer objects, just to drive from A to B. We build mobile works of art.
Author: Chris Bangle

Quote: My heart only grows more powerful the more my flowing love goes unabsorbed, unobserved, and unappreciated.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: At the end of the day, all you can hope for is to go on. The older I get, the more I realize that just keeping on keeping on is what life's all about.
Author: Janis Ian

Quote: Smoke veils the air like souls in drifting suspension, declining the war's insistence everyone move on.
Author: Jayne Anne Phillips

Quote: A starving man asleep dreams of food, and even a crumb becomes a feast. When you're hungry for affection even a no is better than being ignored.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: When the sun is shining I can do anything; no mountain is too high, no trouble too difficult to overcome.
Author: Wilma Rudolph

Quote: I'm the one that's got to die when it's time for me to die, so let me live my life the way I want to.
Author: Jimi Hendrix
Origin: Jimi Hendrix - Axis: Bold as Love

Quote: In examining disease, we gain wisdom about anatomy and physiology and biology. In examining the person with disease, we gain wisdom about life.
Author: Oliver Sacks

Quote: Love is a snowmobile racing across the tundra and then suddenly it flips over, pinning you underneath. At night, the ice weasels come.
Author: Matt Groening

Quote: Love is not affectionate feeling, but a steady wish for the loved person's ultimate good as far as it can be obtained.
Author: C.S. Lewis

Quote: Sometimes new opportunity means new opposition. Not everything God asks us to do will be comfortable.
Author: Joyce Meyer

Quote: Institutionalized rejection of difference is an absolute necessity in a profit economy which needs outsiders as surplus people.
Author: Audre Lorde

Quote: Only a fool tells people that only fools buy gold. In the coming future, I'd rather have fool's gold than dollar bills. Ask Zimbabwe.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: Any happiness, no matter how brief, seemed better than the long, simmering torture of waking up day after day, knowing I could never have him.
Author: Becca Fitzpatrick

Quote: I will never buy a fish tank, because I don't believe in supporting the funding of aquatic war machines.
Author: Jarod Kintz

Quote: Almost all the time, you tell yourself you're loving somebody when you're just using them. This only looks like love.
Author: Chuck Palahniuk

Quote: I still remember her meandering Mississippi kiss. I sipped it like a riverboat captain in the desert. Ah, to be young and naughtily nautical.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: At even one penny, this book would be overpriced. In fact, free is too expensive, because you'd still waste time by reading it.

Quote: Why pay for an undesirable outcome with someone else? For the same money, you could have paid me to stay at home and do nothing.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: When we lose one we love, our bitterest tears are called forth by the memory of hours when we loved not enough.
Author: Maurice Maeterlinck

Quote: Life is not lost by dying; life is lost minute by minute, day by dragging day, in all the thousand small uncaring ways.
Author: Stephen Vincent Benet

Quote: And, drunk with my own madness, I shouted at him furiously, "Make life beautiful! Make life beautiful!
Author: Charles Baudelaire

Quote: Yes, reason has been a part of organized religion, ever since two nudists took dietary advice from a talking snake.
Author: Jon Stewart

Quote: In the end, it is our defiance that redeems us. If wolves had a religion - if there was a religion of the wolf - that it is what it would tell us.
Author: Mark Rowlands
Origin: The Philosopher and the Wolf: Lessons from the Wild on Love, Death, and Happiness

Quote: There is no dishonor in losing the race. There is only dishonor in not racing because you are afraid to lose.
Author: Garth Stein
Origin: The Art of Racing in the Rain

Quote: One of the few things in life that cannot possibly do harm in the end is the honest pursuit of the truth.
Author: Peter Kreeft

Quote: I'm a romantic. I like cold coffee and orgasms that arrive fifteen minutes after I've put on my clothes and paid the lady.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: A Story That Talks about Talking Is Like Chatter to Chattering Teeth, and Every Set of Dentures Can Attest to the Fact That No..

Quote: When the last dime is gone, I'll sit on the curb outside with a pencil and a ten cent notebook and start the whole thing over again.
Author: Preston Sturges

Quote: The five essential entrepreneurial skills for success: Concentration, Discrimination, Organization, Innovation and Communication.
Author: Harold S. Geneen

Quote: He who would learn to fly one day must first learn to walk and run and climb and dance; one cannot fly into flying.
Author: Friedrich Nietzsche

Quote: If for some reason we get separated, I want you to wait for me. I'll find you, I promise. - Gabriel Emerson
Author: Sylvain Reynard

Quote: If you can learn to love yourself and all the flaws, you can love other people so much better. And that makes you so happy.
Author: Kristin Chenoweth

Quote: My words are the kisses but many of the times they don't taste so good as the touch of your lips does to mine.
Author: Santosh Kalwar

Quote: You have to be able to center yourself, to let all of your emotions go. Don't ever forget that you play with your soul as well as your body.
Author: Kareem Abdul- Jabbar

Quote: Censorship of anything, at any time, in any place, on whatever pretense, has always been and always will be the last resort of the boob and the bigot.
Author: Eugene O'Neill

Quote: In terms of doing things I take a fairly scientific approach to why things happen and how they happen. I don't know if there's a god or not...
Author: Bill Gates

Quote: There is safety in numbers. And science. Clone your way to being safe. Nobody can protect you like you. And you and you and you.
Author: Jarod Kintz

Quote: Clouds come floating into my life, no longer to carry rain or usher storm, but to add color to my sunset sky.
Author: Rabindranath Tagore

Quote: What is existence for but to be laughed at if men in their twenties have already attained the utmost?
Author: Søren Kierkegaard
Origin: Either/ Or: A Fragment of Life

Quote: God is the most beautiful, and beauty is the expression of God. If you can't appreciate beauty in the world how can you understand God?
Author: Amit Ray

Quote: Love is not blind; that is the last thing that it is. Love is bound; and the more it is bound the less it is blind.
Author: G.K. Chesterton

Quote: The tide was a poem that only time could create, and I watched it stream and brim and makes its steady dash homeward, to the ocean.
Author: Pat Conroy

Quote: The atheist staring from his attic window is often nearer to God than the believer caught up in his own false image of God.
Author: Martin Buber

Quote: My story was not the story of a girl's broken dreams: it was the story of a girl who couldn't be broken.
Author: Heather Schuck

Quote: As my father used to tell me, the only true sign of success in life is being able to do for a living that which makes you happy.
Author: Al Yankovic

Quote: To some education is just a bore; to most education is food for the brain and enrichment for the present and future.
Author: Ana Monnar

Quote: You can get through very serious and sometimes horrible and sometimes embarrassing and very awkward situations with humor. It gives us a way out.
Author: Janet Evanovich

Quote: Can America get back to a point where politicians are honest? Not unless that point is the tip of a sword.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: Who Moved My Choose?: An Amazing Way to Deal With Change by Deciding to Let Indecision Into Your Life

Quote: Success means having the courage, the determination, and the will to become the person you believe you were meant to be.
Author: George A. Sheehan

Quote: When something needs to be said, you look for a man to say it. But when something needs actually to be done, you look for a woman.
Author: P.B. Kerr

Quote: We have to endure the discordance between imagination and fact. It is better to say, "I am suffering," than to say, "This landscape is ugly.
Author: Simone Weil

Quote: I want love to conquer all. But love can't conquer anything. It can't do anything on it's own. It relies on us to do the conquering on its behalf.
Author: David Levithan

Quote: There is freedom waiting for you, On the breezes of the sky, And you ask "What if I fall?" Oh but my darling, What if you fly?
Author: Erin Hanson

Quote: I just hope people see that if God can do something beautiful with my broken pieces, then God truly has a plan for each and everyone of us.
Author: Nick Vujicic

Quote: Some ghosts or felt presences may simply be the essence of another living person projected outward while sleeping.
Author: Doug Dillon

Quote: If you don't think you need to develop your personal brand, my advice for you comes in the wisdom of two road signs: STOP; DEAD END.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: This Book is Not FOR SALE

Quote: How can a society that exists on instant mashed potatoes, packaged cake mixes, frozen dinners, and instant cameras teach patience to its young?
Author: Paul Sweeney

Quote: It occurred to me by intuition, and music was the driving force behind that intuition. My discovery was the result of musical perception.
Author: Albert Einstein

Quote: Tests were always easy for me. I saw them as games, saw myself as being in a contest against a mythical adversary, and welcomed the challenge.
Author: Walter Dean Myers

Quote: It is important to our friends to believe that we are unreservedly frank with them, and important to friendship that we are not.
Author: Mignon McLaughlin

Quote: You would not want to be responsible for someone else's happiness, so please do not hold someone else responsible for yours!
Author: Jennifer O'Neill

Quote: The body grows slowly and steadily but the soul grows by leaps and bounds. It may come to its full stature in an hour.
Author: L.M. Montgomery

Quote: No matter how much you think you love somebody, you'll step back when the pool of their blood edges up too close.
Author: Chuck Palahniuk

Quote: Do what you love; you'll be better at it. It sounds pretty simple, but you'd be surprised how many people don't get this one right away.
Author: LL Cool J

Quote: The art of effective listening is essential to clear communication, and clear communication is necessary to management success.
Author: James Cash Penney

Quote: Journalists say a thing that they know isn't true, in the hope that if they keep on saying it long enough it will be true.
Author: Arnold Bennett

Quote: It's much easier to not know things sometimes. Things change and friends leave. And life doesn't stop for anybody.
Author: Stephen Chbosky
Origin: The Perks of Being a Wallflower

Quote: I don't do that kind of negativity. If you put your energy into thinking about how much the fall would hurt, you're already halfway down.
Author: Tana French

Quote: The optimist proclaims that we live in the best of all possible worlds; and the pessimist fears this is true.
Author: James Branch Cabell

Quote: You can't go around hoping that most people have sterling moral characters. The most you can hope for is that people will pretend that they do.
Author: Fran Lebowitz

Quote: I don't know much more than I did when I was alive. Most of the stuff I know now that I didn't know then I can't put into words.
Author: Neil Gaiman

Quote: You know you're in love when you can't fall asleep because reality is finally better than your dreams.
Author: Dr. Seuss

Quote: I carry death in my left pocket. Sometimes I take it out and talk to it: "Hello, baby, how you doing? When you coming for me? I'll be ready.
Author: Charles Bukowski

Quote: But this room looked like it had been decorated by the unholy lovechild of Barbie and Strawberry Shortcake.
Author: Rachel Hawkins

Quote: The master said You must write what you see. But what I see does not move me. The master answered Change what you see.
Author: Louise Glück

Quote: Know you not that a good man does nothing for appearance sake, but for the sake of having done right?
Author: Epictetus

Quote: Most of the important things in the world have been accomplished by people who have kept on trying when there seemed to be no hope at all.
Author: Dale Carnegie

Quote: All things are inconstant except the faith in the soul, which changes all things and fills their inconstancy with light.
Author: James Joyce

Quote: When you choose one way out of many, all the ways you don't take are snuffed out like candles, as if they'd never existed.
Author: Philip Pullman

Quote: Any life is made up of a single moment, the moment in which a man finds out, once and for all, who he is.
Author: Jorge Luis Borges

Quote: The pleasure of criticizing takes away from us the pleasure of being moved by some very fine things.
Author: Jean de La Bruyère

Quote: There are no rules for friendship. It must be left to itself. We cannot force it any more than love.
Author: William Hazlitt

Quote: Suffering passes, while love is eternal. That's a gift that you have received from God. Don't waste it.
Author: Laura Ingalls Wilder

Quote: Once in a while it really hits people that they don't have to experience the world in the way they have been told to.
Author: Alan Keightley

Quote: Life is all memory, except for the one present moment that goes by you so quickly you hardly catch it going.
Author: Tennessee Williams

Quote: Beauty is not who you are on the outside, it is the wisdom and time you gave away to save another struggling soul, like you.
Author: Shannon L. Alder

Quote: when man determined to destroy himself he picked the was of shall and finding only why smashed it into because
Author: E.E. Cummings

Quote: Can you do it? When the time comes? When the time comes there will be no time. Now is the time. Curse God and die.
Author: Cormac McCarthy

Quote: If you are unclear about what attitudes you adhere to, you will have a difficult time evaluation which ones are not serving your highest good.
Author: Deborah Day

Quote: And then his noise falls completely silent- And he stops struggling- And looking right into my eyes- He dies. My Todd dies.
Author: Patrick Ness

Quote: Poetry, plays, novels, music, they are the cry of the human spirit trying to understand itself and make sense of our world.
Author: L.M. Elliott

Quote: Neither of the two people in the room paid any attention to the way I came in, although only one of them was dead.
Author: Raymond Chandler

Quote: It's funny, for all it took was a broken heart and that alone was enough, enough for her to do everything she ever dreamed of.
Author: Robert M. Drake

Quote: The human soul has still greater need of the ideal than of the real. It is by the real that we exist, it is by the ideal that we live.
Author: Victor Hugo

Quote: We cry coming into the world, as everyone around us laughs with joy. And we laugh with joy leaving this world, when everyone around us cries.
Author: Wendy Corsi Staub

Quote: It is a curious thought, but it is only when you see people looking ridiculous that you realize just how much you love them.
Author: Agatha Christie

Quote: I wanted to be his life preserver, the thing that would keep him afloat. Instead, he became my anchor. And I'm tired of drowning.
Author: Amanda Grace

Quote: Early in life I had to choose between honest arrogance and hypocritical humility. I chose the former and have seen no reason to change.
Author: Frank Lloyd Wright

Quote: So come to the pond, or the river of your imagination, or the harbor of your longing, and put your lips to the world. And live your life.
Author: Mary Oliver

Quote: ...it's not just learning that's important. It's learning what to do with what you learn and learning why you learn things that matters.
Author: Norton Juster

Quote: A string of burglaries is a crime necklace. Everything I have can be stolen except love, because I give it all away.
Author: Jarod Kintz
Origin: Love quotes for the ages. Specifically ages 18-81.

Quote: Every day we should hear at least one little song, read one good poem, see one exquisite picture, and, if possible, speak a few sensible words.
Author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

Quote: Life is the art of being well deceived; and in order that the deception may succeed it must be habitual and uninterrupted.
Author: William Hazlitt

Quote: Exercises are like prose, whereas yoga is the poetry of movements. Once you understand the grammar of yoga; you can write your poetry of movements.
Author: Amit Ray
Origin: Yoga and Vipassana: An Integrated Life Style

Quote: Life is a mystery- mystery of beauty, bliss and divinity. Meditation is the art of unfolding that mystery.
Author: Amit Ray

Quote: Flowers always make people better, happier, and more helpful; they are sunshine, food and medicine to the mind.
Author: Luther Burbank

Quote: Comparing yourself to others does nothing for you. Society has conditioned us to value people who fit a secular mold of perfection".
Author: Michele Woolley

Quote: For what is it to die but to stand naked in the wind and to melt into the sun? And when the earth shall claim your limbs, then shall you truly dance.
Author: Kahlil Gibran

Quote: The first rule is to keep an untroubled spirit. The second is to look things in the face and know them for what they are.
Author: Marcus Aurelius

Quote: Fear is a probability by its own cause of actions, if we live by it we lose all the possibilities of endurance
Author: Saleem Durrani

Quote: One says the things which one feels the need to say, and which the other will not understand: one speaks for oneself alone.
Author: Marcel Proust

Quote: He liked the mere act of reading, the magic of turning scratches on a page into words inside his head.
Author: John Green

Quote: If the moon smiled, she would resemble you. You leave the same impression Of something beautiful, but annihilating.
Author: Sylvia Plath

Quote: It is the imagination that argues for the Divine Spark within human beings. It is literally a decent of the World's Soul into all of us.
Author: Terence McKenna

Quote: We tend to think of future inventions and discoveries, but in reality, these things already exist in the knowledge bank of the universe.
Author: Stephen Richards

Quote: There are in fact two things, science and opinion; the former begets knowledge, the latter ignorance.
Author: Hippocrates

Quote: I touch you knowing we weren't born tomorrow, and somehow, each of us will help the other live, and somewhere, each of us must help the other die.
Author: Adrienne Rich

Quote: We should never wait for science to give us permission to do the uncommon; if we do, then we are turning science into another religion.
Author: Joe Dispenza
Origin: Breaking The Habit of Being Yourself: How to Lose Your Mind and Create a New One

Quote: Life is always going to be stranger than fiction, because fiction has to be convincing, and life doesn't.
Author: Neil Gaiman

Quote: If only you can add just one great and distinctive thing to your life each day, you shall surely not just live and leave one great thing!
Author: Ernest Agyemang Yeboah

Quote: I like to vote, but not be voted on. I don't mind losing one on one, but to lose through a vote means the majority think I'm a loser.
Author: Jarod Kintz

Quote: Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate; only love can do that.
Author: Martin Luther King

Quote: It seems, in fact, as though the second half of a man's life is made up of nothing, but the habits he has accumulated during the first half.
Author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky

Quote: Whining is not only graceless, but can be dangerous. It can alert a brute that a victim is in the neighborhood.
Author: Maya Angelou
Origin: Wouldn't Take Nothing for My Journey Now

Quote: It's funny whom we end up choosing to love and who ends up choosing to love us. It's rarely the people we think it should be.
Author: Robin Jones Gunn

Quote: In the sky there are always answers and explanations for everything: every pain, every suffering, joy and confusion.
Author: Ishmael Beah
Origin: A Long Way Gone: Memoirs of a Boy Soldier

Quote: None of us knows what might happen even the next minute, yet still we go forward. Because we trust. Because we have Faith.
Author: Paulo Coelho

Quote: Some books should be tasted, some devoured, but only a few should be chewed and digested thoroughly.
Author: Francis Bacon

Quote: You did not just say that. I have a feeling we're on the verge of hugging and coming up with cute nicknames for each other.
Author: Richelle Mead

Quote: If you want good luck; you must go out there and search for that luck!! Because luck is waiting for you to look for it.
Author: Temitope Owosela

Quote: I shall look at you out of the corner of my eye, and you will say nothing. Words are the source of misunderstandings.
Author: Antoine de Saint- Exupéry

Quote: When we face pain in relationships our first response is often to sever bonds rather than to maintain commitment.
Author: bell hooks

Quote: Be not afraid of life. Believe that life is worth living, and your belief will help create the fact.
Author: William James
Origin: The Will to Believe and Other Essays in Popular Philosophy

Quote: Humor comes in all forms, and everyone has their cup of tea about what makes them laugh. But the day we censor humor is a sad one for sure.
Author: Marlee Matlin

Quote: The work of art assumes the existence of the perfect spectator, and is indifferent to the fact that no such person exists.
Author: E. M. Forster

Quote: When humor works, it works because it's clarifying what people already feel. It has to come from someplace real.
Author: Tina Fey

Quote: Love has no culture, boundaries, race and religion. It is pure and beautiful like early morning sunrise falling in lake.
Author: Santosh Kalwar

Quote: (About changing faith) At our best, Christians embrace it, leaving enough space within orthodoxy for God to surprise us every now and then.
Author: Rachel Held Evans
Origin: Evolving in Monkey Town: How a Girl Who Knew All the Answers Learned to Ask the Questions

Quote: And if you say that's because you lot barged into her home like a herd of mentally deficient sheep, I'm disowning all three of you.
Author: Julia Quinn

Quote: The only thing standing between you and your goal is the bullshit story you keep telling yourself as to why you can't achieve it.
Author: Jordan Belfort

Quote: Thought is the original source of all wealth, all success, all material gain, all great discoveries and inventions, and of all achievement.
Author: Claude M. Bristol

Quote: It sounded like walls tumbling, liberty bells chiming, government buildings being stormed. It sounded like a revolution. It sounded like hope.
Author: Alex Scarrow

Quote: Each friend represents a world in us, a world not born until they arrive, and it is only by this meeting that a new world is born.
Author: Anais Nin

Quote: Leonard Cohen has a way with words and with humor that remind me to lighten up, which I appreciate very much.
Author: Damien Rice

Quote: Everything can be explained to the people, on the single condition that you want them to understand.
Author: Frantz Fanon

Quote: Nothing ever invented provides such sustenance, such infinite reward for time spent, as a good book.
Author: David McCullough

Quote: I hunger for your sleek laugh and your hands the color of a furious harvest. I want to eat the sunbeams flaring in your beauty.
Author: Pablo Neruda

Quote: I hope that one day you will have the experience of doing something you do not understand for someone you love.
Author: Jonathan Safran Foer

Quote: Something about being rejected at Church Camp felt so much more awful than being rejected at school.
Author: Craig Thompson

Quote: He stepped down, trying not to look long at her, as if she were the sun, yet he saw her, like the sun, even without looking.
Author: Leo Tolstoy
